title: Wikipedia
text: Wikipedia is a free online encyclopedia written and maintained by volunteer contributors. It is hosted by the Wikimedia Foundation and contains articles on many subjects in many languages.

title: Python programming language
text: Python is a high-level programming language known for readable syntax and a large standard library. It is often used for web development, data analysis, automation, and artificial intelligence.

title: Vancouver
text: Vancouver is a coastal city in British Columbia, Canada. It is known for its port, mountain and ocean setting, film production, and diverse urban population.

title: Artificial intelligence
text: Artificial intelligence, often abbreviated AI, is the field of building computer systems that perform tasks associated with human intelligence, such as language understanding, reasoning, planning, and pattern recognition.

title: FastAPI
text: FastAPI is a modern Python web framework for building APIs. It uses Python type hints, supports automatic validation with Pydantic, and can generate OpenAPI documentation.

title: Ollama
text: Ollama is software for running large language models on a local computer. It provides a local API and can manage model downloads and execution.

title: Retrieval-augmented generation
text: Retrieval-augmented generation is a technique that gives a language model external context retrieved from a document collection before it writes an answer.

title: Speech recognition
text: Speech recognition is the process of converting spoken language into text. It is used in dictation systems, voice assistants, captions, and accessibility tools.

title: Text-to-speech
text: Text-to-speech is technology that converts written text into spoken audio. It is used for screen readers, navigation systems, announcements, and voice interfaces.

title: British Columbia
text: British Columbia is a province on the west coast of Canada. Victoria is the provincial capital, and Vancouver is the province's largest city.
